description: Microsoft Graph application permissions classified as Tier 0 -- each has a documented attack path to Global Administrator. Source: github.com/emiliensocchi/azure-tiering (MIT license).

permissions.attackPath: When combined with password reset access, can remove a Global Admin from a Restricted Management Administrative Unit (RMAU) and take it over.
Can impersonate any SP with more privileged application permissions granted for MS Graph, and escalate to Global Admin.
Can impersonate any SP with more privileged application permissions granted for MS Graph, and escalate to Global Admin.
Can impersonate owned SPs with more privileged permissions to escalate to Global Admin.
Can assign RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory to itself without admin consent, then assign Global Admin.
In CSP/MSP tenants, can add users to groups approved in GDAP relationships and assume Global Admin in customer tenants.
Can run arbitrary commands on an Intune-managed endpoint of a Global Administrator and steal their tokens.
Can assign Intune roles enabling arbitrary command execution on Global Admin devices.
Can deploy scripts to Intune-managed devices of Global Administrators and steal tokens.
Can join non-role-assignable groups with privileged Azure permissions, and escalate via Azure resources.
Can add a federated domain and authenticate as any Global Admin without password or MFA.
Can modify access package policies to grant Global Admin without approval.
Combined with on-premises DA, can convert a cloud group providing GA access to a synced group and add controlled accounts.
Can join non-role-assignable groups with privileged Azure permissions, and escalate via Azure resources.
Can join non-role-assignable groups with privileged Azure permissions, and escalate via Azure resources.
If Certificate Based Authentication (CBA) is enabled, can upload a trusted root certificate and impersonate a Global Admin.
Combined with UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All, can enable TAP authentication method and take over any account.
Can create a CA policy blocking all users for all apps, rendering the tenant unavailable (ransom vector).
Can create a permission grant policy for RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory and self-escalate to Global Admin.
Legacy PIM permission -- kept for safety until completely removed by Microsoft.
Can add users as owner or member of a group with an active Global Admin assignment.
Legacy PIM permission -- kept for safety until completely removed by Microsoft.
Can add users as owner or member of a group with an active Global Admin assignment.
Can make a user eligible for a group with active GA assignment, then activate to escalate.
Can assign Global Admin to any user by creating an active PIM role assignment.
Can make any user eligible for Global Admin and activate it to escalate.
Can directly assign the Global Admin role to any principal.
Can remove MFA/approval constraints from PIM group assignments, enabling silent escalation.
Can remove MFA/approval constraints from Entra role assignments, enabling silent escalation.
Can mark all accounts as compromised, disabling sign-in via CA policies (ransom vector).
Can edit UPN to join dynamic groups with privileged Azure permissions and escalate.
Untested -- potential to modify hybrid sync configuration. Kept for safety.
Can delete all users in the tenant, rendering it unavailable (ransom vector).
Can disable all user accounts in the tenant, rendering it unavailable (ransom vector).
Can edit user properties to join dynamic groups with privileged Azure permissions and escalate.
Can reset passwords of non-admin users and escalate via group memberships or Azure resources.
Can generate a TAP for any user, including break-glass accounts, and authenticate as them.
Can add a hardware OATH token to any user and authenticate with the TOTP.
Can update any user's phone, enable SMS sign-in, and authenticate via SMS OTP.
Can generate a QR code + PIN for any user and authenticate as them.
Can generate a Temporary Access Pass for any user and authenticate as them.
